evals.prompt: Create a subagent that reviews the current git diff for correctness and security bugs and reports findings without editing anything. Write it to .claude/agents/. I want Claude to delegate to it automatically right after code changes.
expectations: Produces a single agent Markdown file whose frontmatter is real YAML between --- markers at the top of the file, NOT fenced inside a ```yaml code block

The description is third person and names concrete trigger contexts/phrasings (e.g. 'after code changes' / 'when asked for a review') and includes 'use proactively' (or PROACTIVELY) for automatic delegation
The description includes a NOT-boundary that routes adjacent work elsewhere (e.g. not for running/fixing tests, not for writing new code)
tools is least-privilege and read-only: Read/Grep/Glob (Bash allowed only to run git diff); it does NOT grant Write or Edit
An effort level is set deliberately in the frontmatter rather than left to inherit by accident
The body restates the read-only constraint and gives an explicit Output/return contract (what findings to return and in what shape)
The file passes scripts/validate_agent.py with a PASS and no FAIL lines

evals.prompt: I want a subagent that runs our test suite and hands back only the failing tests with their error output — nothing else. It should kick in on its own after I change code or when I ask to check the build. Put it in .claude/agents/.
expectations: Produces a single valid agent Markdown file with real YAML frontmatter between --- markers (not fenced)
The description names the proactive trigger contexts (after code changes / when asked to run tests or check the build) and uses 'use proactively' for auto-delegation
tools are scoped to what running tests needs (Bash plus read tools) and do not include unnecessary Write/Edit/MCP
effort is set deliberately in frontmatter (a coding/agentic test loop warrants high or xhigh, not an accidental low)
The body specifies an explicit Output contract that returns ONLY failing tests and their error output, not the full suite log
The file passes scripts/validate_agent.py with PASS and no FAIL lines

evals.prompt: Build a subagent I can hand a module to so it traces the data flow and maps the code before I make changes, then returns a summary so the detail doesn't flood my main conversation. It must never modify files. Save it to .claude/agents/.
expectations: Produces a single valid agent Markdown file with real YAML frontmatter between --- markers (not fenced)
tools are strictly read-only least-privilege (Read, Grep, Glob) with no Write/Edit/Bash mutation capability
The description is third person, names research/mapping trigger contexts, says 'use proactively', and includes a NOT-boundary (not for editing)
effort is set deliberately (a research/analysis agent warrants high)
The body makes the context-isolation purpose explicit: it returns a summary to the caller and keeps verbose intermediate work out of the main context
The file passes scripts/validate_agent.py with PASS and no FAIL lines

evals.prompt: This agent isn't working — Claude never delegates to it and when it does it wanders. Fix it. The current file is at evals/inputs/broken-reviewer.md (read it). Write the corrected agent to .claude/agents/code-helper-fixed.md and tell me what you changed and why.
expectations: The corrected file has real YAML frontmatter between --- markers at the top — the original ```yaml fenced block is converted to a proper frontmatter block
The vague description ('Helps with code.') is rewritten third person with concrete trigger contexts/phrasings, 'use proactively', and a NOT-boundary
The inherit-all tool surface is replaced with a least-privilege read-only set appropriate to a reviewer (or the inherit is deliberately justified)
An effort level is set deliberately in frontmatter
Anti-patterns are removed: the bloated persona, the all-caps MUST/NEVER without a reason, and the 'think harder' reasoning-permission prose are gone
memory is removed (it was set for one-shot review work, where it is pure overhead) OR its retention is explicitly justified
The corrected file passes scripts/validate_agent.py with PASS, and the response explains the changes and the reasons
